{{Expand Japanese}}
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:月田古墳群]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:月田古墳群]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:月田古墳群]]
{{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Maebashi|WD=Q201613|1=Maebashi}}}}
'''Tsukida Kofun Cluster''' (月田古墳群) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) from the late [[Kofun period]] located in {{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|WD=Q201613|lt=Maebashi|1=Maebashi}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}, Japan, specifically in {{ill|Kasukawa Town Tsukida|ja|粕川町月田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasukawa Town Tsukida|1=Q22123330|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22123330}} and {{ill|Kasukawa Town Murozawa|ja|粕川町室沢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasukawa Town Murozawa|1=Q22123417|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22123417}}.

== Overview ==
The Tsukida Kofun Cluster comprises 35–40 Kofuns located in {{ill|Kasukawa Town Tsukida|ja|粕川町月田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasukawa Town Tsukida|1=Q22123330|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22123330}} and {{ill|Kasukawa Town Murozawa|ja|粕川町室沢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasukawa Town Murozawa|1=Q22123417|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22123417}} in Maebashi City, but most of these have already been destroyed.{{Sfn|Kojima|1982|loc=序}}{{Sfn|Kasukawa Village History Compilation Committee|1972|p=35}} Between 1948 and 1955, six mounds were excavated by {{ill|Kisao Ozaki|ja|尾崎喜左雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisao Ozaki|1=Kisao Ozaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21698487}} and the Gunma University Archaeology Department.{{Sfn|Kojima|1982|p=16}} These mounds were dated from the late 6th century to the late 7th century.{{Sfn|Kasukawa Village History Compilation Committee|1972|p=48}} Later investigations in 1980–1981 by the Kasukawa Village Education Board indicated a predominance of mounds constructed in the late 7th century.{{Sfn|Kojima|1982|p=16}}

Two kofun in the cluster have been designated as prefectural historic sites:
* '''Kagamitezuka Kofun''' - Gunma Prefecture Historic Site (designated February 8, 1949)
* '''Danzuka Kofun''' - Gunma Prefecture Historic Site (designated October 5, 1951){{Cite web|title=List of Prefectural Cultural Properties|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5107.html|website=City of Maebashi|access-date=2024-04-21|language=ja}}

== Surrounding Area ==
* {{ill|Chikado Shrine (Maebashi)|ja|近戸神社 (前橋市粕川町月田)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikado Shrine (Maebashi)|1=Chikado Shrine (Maebashi)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131275526}}
* {{ill|Yamashiro Multi-story Tower|ja|山上多重塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Multi-story Tower|1=Yamakami Multi-storied Pagoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122341137}}

== References ==
{{Reflist}}

== Bibliography ==
* {{Cite book|editor=Kasukawa Village History Compilation Committee|title=Kasukawa Village History|origdate=1972-12-10|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/101858|doi=10.24484/sitereports.101858}}
* {{Cite book|author=Junichi Kojima|title=Tsukida Kofun Cluster B1|origdate=1982-03|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/15291|doi=10.24484/sitereports.15291}}

{{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{Pref-stub|pref=Gunma Prefecture}}

{{Kofun navbar}}

{{translated page|ja|月田古墳群|version=101730633|comment=Imported full ja history}}



[[ja:月田古墳群]]

<!--Tsukida Kofun Group--><!--Tsukida Kofun Group-->
<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis-->


{{wikidata link|Q85880981}}
[[Category:Designated historic sites of Gunma Prefecture]]
[[Category:Image requests in Maebashi City]]
[[Category:Kofun clusters]]
[[Category:Kofun of Gunma prefecture]]
[[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
